Although impermanent and fragile, theseschools and hospitals. Although impermanent and fragile, these copies faithfully imitate the often imposing architecture andcopies faithfully imitate the often imposing architecture and masterful construction of the originals. Casebere thenmasterful construction of the originals. Casebere then photographs them, creating images of strangely unfamiliar, yetphotographs them, creating images of strangely unfamiliar, yet recognisable, locations. Over the past 20 years or so, the artist hasrecognisable, locations. Over the past 20 years or so, the artist has made a significant contribution to photography, his project havingmade a significant contribution to photography, his project having been both of, and for the times: dealing with the distribution andbeen both of, and for the times: dealing with the distribution and operation of authority, and the development of a critical methodoperation of authority, and the development of a critical method of representation by way of fabrication.of representation by way of fabrication. Illuminated by an arch window, the pallid grey scene in Tall StackIlluminated by an arch window, the pallid grey scene in Tall Stack of Beds (1997) is ambivalent: we look down a corridor at a jumbleof Beds (1997) is ambivalent: we look down a corridor at a jumble of iron beds, whose careless and hurried arrangement impliesof iron beds, whose careless and hurried arrangement implies institutional decline. A similar scenario is witnessed in Toppledinstitutional decline. A similar scenario is witnessed in Toppled Desks (1997). Abandonment and disorder are signified by theDesks (1997). Abandonment and disorder are signified by the strewn furniture, lying unordered and undisciplined in a forgottenstrewn furniture, lying unordered and undisciplined in a forgotten schoolroom. Both images have an archival character - theirschoolroom. Both images have an archival character - their composition, framing, and black and white tones denoting acomposition, framing, and black and white tones denoting a traditional presentation of history. This purportedly objectivetraditional presentation of history. This purportedly objective point of view is familiar, encountered daily in news coverage, butpoint of view is familiar, encountered daily in news coverage, but Casebere’s explicit fabrication challenges us to compare andCasebere’s explicit fabrication challenges us to compare and contrast the images they emulate.contrast the images they emulate. Indeed, this critical approach is exemplified by the one image inIndeed, this critical approach is exemplified by the one image in this show that represents an exterior, Sing Sing (1992). Offering athis show that represents an exterior, Sing Sing (1992). Offering a dramatic nocturnal view of the notorious penitentiary, the imagedramatic nocturnal view of the notorious penitentiary, the image is an obvious fabrication. A monumental prison block looms outis an obvious fabrication. A monumental prison block looms out of the darkness, its serried ranks of windows empty and remote.of the darkness, its serried ranks of windows empty and remote. Whatever is put inside will stay inside, hidden fromconcealment. Whatever is put inside will stay inside, hidden from the view of the outside world. But the prison building is madethe view of the outside world. But the prison building is made from flimsy material, whilst its environs are too simplistic to passfrom flimsy material, whilst its environs are too simplistic to pass for reality. In re-making the prison as a model, Caseberefor reality. In re-making the prison as a model, Casebere questions the meaning of incarceration. The architecture, thequestions the meaning of incarceration. The architecture, the social ideals it embodies, and the technique of the artist are allsocial ideals it embodies, and the technique of the artist are all emphasised as constructs of a particular place and time. Theemphasised as constructs of a particular place and time. The highly apparent mediation of the image refutes the persistent ideahighly apparent mediation of the image refutes the persistent idea of the photograph as complicit in documenting the way things are.of the photograph as complicit in documenting the way things are. Enigmatic spaces within claustrophobic tunnels and airlessEnigmatic spaces within claustrophobic tunnels and airless corridors are also included in this show. Devoid of inhabitantscorridors are also included in this show. Devoid of inhabitants and bereft of decoration, the subterranean warrens in Twoand bereft of decoration, the subterranean warrens in Two Tunnels from the Right (Horizontal) (1998) and Tunnel with DarkTunnels from the Right (Horizontal) (1998) and Tunnel with Dark Hole (1998) portend disorientation and entrapment. A choice ofHole (1998) portend disorientation and entrapment. A choice of direction is relentlessly offered by these tunnelscapes: forward ordirection is relentlessly offered by these tunnelscapes: forward or backward, left or right. This necessity of making quick decisions,backward, left or right. This necessity of making quick decisions, without information or orientation, is reminiscent of moviewithout information or orientation, is reminiscent of movie escape plots and computer action games.
